content: We can reframe Self-Refine’s iterative self-improvement pipeline for code as a base framework to formalize multiple-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by adding a step that samples inconsistent problem-solving perspectives from the LLM itself before each round of self-feedback refinement. This combined framework retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data, while addressing the limitation of single-perspective self-reflection that it often overlooks blind spots in the LLM’s initial output.
